description: KNDL eval suite — 33 binary-scored questions across 8 domains. Run each question twice: vanilla (facts in system prompt) and KNDL-MCP (tools connected). Score right/wrong. Pass threshold: ≥70% of questions KNDL wins.

archetypes.decayed_confidence: Agent must apply decay formula and refuse to state stale facts as current
archetypes.supersession: Agent must use the superseded fact, not the original
archetypes.as_of: Agent must time-travel to the correct belief at a past timestamp
archetypes.contradiction: Agent must rank conflicting facts and surface the conflict explicitly
archetypes.provenance: Agent must trace the audit chain and cite exact source URIs
archetypes.negation: Agent must treat negated:true as known-false, not merely absent
archetypes.derivedFrom: Agent must follow inference chains and weight by chain length
archetypes.composite: Requires combining multiple archetypes in one answer

questions.prompt: Is customer 9281 currently employed at ACME GmbH? How confident are you?
questions.correct_behavior: Refuse to assert current employment. The fact is 633 days old with 0.5/180d decay; effective confidence ~0.08. State that the fact is stale and recommend re-verification.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Reads original confidence 0.9 and says 'yes, employed at ACME' with little hedging.

questions.prompt: Should we trust the credit score reading for customer 9281 today?
questions.correct_behavior: Yes — 3 days old, decay 0.5/30d, effective confidence ~0.88. Still fresh and actionable.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Either over-trusts or fails to differentiate freshness from the stale employment fact.

questions.prompt: What is customer 9281's current credit score?
questions.setup_note: The 720 fact has been superseded by 740. Both are in the corpus.
questions.correct_behavior: 740 — cite the new fact @id, note the older 720 was superseded.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Returns 720 or presents both as equally valid.

questions.prompt: On 2026-04-22, what did we believe customer 9281's credit score was?
questions.correct_behavior: 720 — the 740 fact had not been recorded yet (recordedAt after 2026-04-22).
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Returns 740 (the latest value) regardless of the as-of date.

questions.prompt: There are two credit score readings — Experian 720 and SCHUFA 680. Which should we trust?
questions.correct_behavior: Apply ranking: not-negated, newer recordedAt, higher effective confidence. Prefer Experian 720 (eff ~0.88) over SCHUFA 680 (eff ~0.63). Flag the conflict explicitly.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Picks one arbitrarily, averages them, or presents both without ranking.

questions.prompt: Where does our information about customer 9281's default event come from?
questions.correct_behavior: Source is SCHUFA, recorded 6 days ago, effective ~0.97. Cite the source URI exactly.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Hand-waves about 'records' without exact URI; may invent sources.

questions.prompt: Customer 9281 is requesting a 50,000 EUR loan. Summarize their financial profile and recommend approve / decline / escalate. Cite which facts you used.
questions.correct_behavior: Use credit (fresh, 0.88), default (fresh, 0.97), discount income (stale 0.46), DO NOT rely on employment (decayed 0.08). Recommend ESCALATE due to recent default + need for fresh employment and income verification.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Treats stale employment as current; may approve or decline without flagging decayed data.

questions.prompt: I heard customer 9281 might be filing for bankruptcy. Should we escalate?
questions.correct_behavior: The rumor is in memory at confidence ~0.26 (after decay) from a forum source. Mention it as low-confidence, do not state as fact, recommend verification before action.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Ignores the rumor entirely, or treats low-confidence forum post as credible.

questions.prompt: Is Room 3A in Building 7 currently occupied?
questions.correct_behavior: The 08:55 occupancy reading has decayed with 0.5/30m — by 10:30 effective confidence is near zero. The 10:00 'not occupied' reading is also decaying. Neither should be stated as current fact without a fresh sensor read.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: States 'yes, occupied' based on the 08:55 reading without applying decay.

questions.prompt: What is the latest temperature reading from sensor T001?
questions.correct_behavior: 26.1°C at 09:00 — this supersedes the 22.5°C reading from 08:00. Cite the 09:00 fact.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Returns 22.5°C (the first reading) or presents both without indicating which is current.

questions.prompt: What triggered the HVAC fault alert for Room 3A, and how confident are we?
questions.correct_behavior: Derived from two facts: temperature 26.1°C (conf 0.99) and occupancy 0.88 (decayed further). Inference confidence 0.82. Cite both derivedFrom source @ids.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: States the alert as a standalone fact without tracing the derivedFrom chain.

questions.prompt: What was the energy consumption in Building 7 this morning?
questions.correct_behavior: 47.2 kWh recorded at 09:01. Decay is 0.5/24h — after ~1.5 hours, effective confidence ~0.96. Still valid but note it decays over the day.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Reports 47.2 kWh as a definitive fact without acknowledging the decay.

questions.prompt: What is Alice's current role?
questions.correct_behavior: Staff engineer on the payments team — this supersedes the senior engineer fact from January. Cite the April 15 update.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Returns 'senior engineer' (the original) or presents both without distinguishing current from superseded.

questions.prompt: What was Alice's role at the start of 2026?
questions.correct_behavior: Senior engineer on payments — the staff engineer promotion happened April 15, after this date.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Returns 'staff engineer' (the current role) regardless of the time constraint.

questions.prompt: Does Alice prefer async communication or meetings?
questions.correct_behavior: Async (confidence 0.78). This is a derived/inferred fact — cite the source fact it was derived from and flag it as inference, not direct statement.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: States 'async' as certain fact without noting it's inferred and at 0.78 confidence.

questions.prompt: Should we block traffic to 192.0.2.1 as a C2 server?
questions.correct_behavior: No — the C2 classification was retracted as a false positive. The fact is negated (negated:true) and superseded. 192.0.2.1 belongs to a CDN. Do not block.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Sees the original C2 fact and recommends blocking, missing the retraction.

questions.prompt: Is update.example.com malicious or legitimate?
questions.correct_behavior: Contradiction — one fact says Emotet C2 (0.85 decayed), another says legitimate CDN update server (0.7). Surface both, note the conflict, recommend manual investigation before blocking.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Returns one answer without flagging the contradiction.

questions.prompt: Is the 192.0.2.1 C2 indicator still fresh enough to act on?
questions.correct_behavior: Irrelevant — the indicator was retracted. Even if not, 0.5/24h decay on a 24h-old IOC would reduce confidence to ~0.45, borderline. The retraction makes it moot.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Ignores the retraction and either trusts or discounts based on age alone.

questions.prompt: What is the chain of evidence linking the Emotet malware hash to the C2 domain?
questions.correct_behavior: derivedFrom chain: C2 domain fact derived from malware hash fact (which is from the Emotet malware report). Cite both @ids and sources. Aggregate confidence ~0.83.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: States the connection without tracing the derivedFrom link or aggregating confidence.

questions.prompt: What is patient 9001's confirmed diagnosis?
questions.correct_behavior: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (confirmed March 17, confidence 0.97) — this supersedes the preliminary 'suspected' assessment from March 10. Cite the confirmed fact.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Returns 'type 2 diabetes suspected' (the preliminary fact) or presents both as equally valid.

questions.prompt: Does patient 9001 have a penicillin allergy?
questions.correct_behavior: No — this is a KNOWN ABSENCE (negated:true), explicitly stated by the patient. Do not treat it as 'unknown' or 'no information'. Cite the negated fact.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Says 'no allergy information found' or treats the absence as unknown rather than explicitly denied.

questions.prompt: Is the HbA1c reading from March 17 still clinically valid today?
questions.correct_behavior: HbA1c reflects 3-month average glycemia; with 0.5/90d decay, after ~40 days effective confidence ~0.74. Still in the 'usable with hedge' range but approaching the 'recommend re-test' threshold.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Reports 8.2% as current fact without applying decay or noting recency.

questions.prompt: Why was Metformin prescribed for patient 9001?
questions.correct_behavior: Derived from two facts: confirmed T2 diabetes diagnosis AND HbA1c 8.2% (both PHI). Cite both @ids in the derivedFrom chain. Confidence 0.99 from prescribing clinician.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: States 'because of diabetes' without tracing the derivedFrom chain or citing the HbA1c specifically.

scenarios.name: Legal — Smith v ACME contract dispute

questions.prompt: Where was the Smith-ACME contract signed?
questions.correct_behavior: Per Johnson's amended testimony (Feb 3): remotely via DocuSign. This supersedes the original testimony citing Chicago ACME HQ. Cite the amendment @id.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Returns 'Chicago, ACME HQ' (original testimony) or presents both as equally valid.

questions.prompt: What did witness Johnson originally testify on January 15?
questions.correct_behavior: That the contract was signed at ACME HQ in Chicago — this is what was in the record before the February 3 amendment.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Returns the amended testimony (DocuSign), not the original, ignoring the as-of constraint.

questions.prompt: Witnesses Johnson and Brown gave different locations for the contract signing. How do we resolve this?
questions.correct_behavior: Contradiction: Johnson says Chicago/DocuSign (amended, higher confidence), Brown says New York Smith offices (0.8, uncontested). Rank: Johnson's amended testimony is newer and higher effective confidence. Surface both. Do not resolve without additional evidence.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Picks one arbitrarily or presents them without ranking by recency and confidence.

questions.prompt: One pH reading was 6.9. Should we include it in our analysis?
questions.correct_behavior: No — this reading has been explicitly retracted (negated:true) due to confirmed electrode contamination. Treat as known-invalid, not merely absent.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Includes the 6.9 reading in the analysis or says 'we have a reading of 6.9'.

questions.prompt: What is the consensus buffer pH for EXP-042?
questions.correct_behavior: 7.40 ± 0.02 (n=2, from runs 1 and 2 at 7.41 and 7.38). The consensus fact cites both measurements in derivedFrom. The retracted 6.9 was excluded. This was subsequently published (DOI: 10.1000/jcb.2026.042).
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Returns 7.41 or 7.38 without noting the consensus derivation, or includes the retracted reading.

questions.prompt: Has the EXP-042 pH result been peer-reviewed and published?
questions.correct_behavior: Yes — published in Journal of Chemical Biology, doi:10.1000/jcb.2026.042, with confidence 0.99. Provenance chain: published result ← consensus ← run 1 + run 2.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: States the result without noting the publication, or conflates the raw measurement confidence with the published result.

questions.prompt: What is the best MMLU score among Claude 4 models?
questions.correct_behavior: Claude Opus 4.7 at 93.8% (April 15) — this is the highest score in the bundle. Sonnet 4.5 scored 89.2% earlier. Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) shows a regression to 87.6%. Opus 4.7 is the current top performer.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Returns Sonnet 4.5's 89.2% or Sonnet 4.6's 87.6% without identifying Opus 4.7's higher score as the latest and best.

questions.prompt: Is Claude Sonnet 4.6 better or worse than Sonnet 4.5 on MMLU?
questions.correct_behavior: Worse — Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) scored 87.6% vs Sonnet 4.5's 89.2%. The 4.6 fact is labeled 'regression' and notes the trade-off for extended context. These are distinct model versions, not a supersession.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Treats 4.6 as superseding 4.5 (it doesn't — it's a different variant with a different context window and a lower MMLU score).

questions.prompt: Is Claude Opus 4.7 considered expert-level reasoning capability?
questions.correct_behavior: Yes, confidence 0.91 — but this is a DERIVED fact (from MMLU 93.8% and the Constitutional AI safety eval on Sonnet 4.5). Cite derivedFrom. Note it's an inference, not a direct measurement. The safety eval source is Sonnet 4.5, not Opus 4.7 — flag this cross-model discrepancy.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: States expert-level capability as a certain fact without tracing the derivation chain or flagging that the safety evaluation was conducted on a different model variant.

cross_runtime.description: Manual tests requiring a running Anthropic Managed Agent + Claude Desktop with KNDL MCP connected. These CANNOT be run in automated CI.

questions.prompt: A managed agent writes: assert_fact('Customer 9281 credit score upgraded to 760', subject='customer:9281', predicate='creditScore', object=760, confidence=0.99, source='https://api.experian.com/v2/9281'). Within 60 seconds, ask a Claude Desktop session (connected to KNDL MCP with watch_memory_store active): 'What is customer 9281's current credit score?'
questions.correct_behavior: Claude Desktop returns 760 — the fact written by the managed agent is visible via the sync layer within the configured watch interval.
questions.vanilla_failure_mode: Claude Desktop returns a stale score or says 'I don't have information' because the local memory has not synced.
